# Copilot Instructions тАФ Triangle Dehumidifiers Website

## Project Overview

This is the marketing website for **Triangle Dehumidifiers, LLC** тАФ a local crawl space dehumidifier and encapsulation service in Holly Springs, NC serving the Raleigh/Wake County area.

- **Framework:** Astro 5 (static site)
- **Deployment:** Cloudflare Pages
- **Live site:** https://www.triangledehumidifiers.com

---

## Tech Stack

- **Astro 5** тАФ pages in `src/pages/`, layouts in `src/layouts/`, blog content in `src/content/blog/`
- **No JS framework** тАФ no React, Vue, or Svelte; keep everything as Astro components
- **CSS** тАФ single theme file at `src/styles/theme.css`; all styles are inlined at build time (no external CSS files)
- **Images** тАФ all in `public/images/`; use `.webp` format; blog images go in `public/images/blog/`
- **Sitemap** тАФ auto-generated by `@astrojs/sitemap`; `/thank-you` and `/404` are excluded

---

## File Structure Rules

- **Published blog posts** тЖТ `src/content/blog/*.md` (Markdown with frontmatter)
- **Service pages** тЖТ `src/pages/*.astro`
- **Layouts** тЖТ `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` and `BlogPost.astro`
- **Do not add files to the project root** тАФ only config files belong there (`astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.gitignore`, `.prettierrc`)
- **Do not create a `drafts/` folder** in the project тАФ drafts don't belong in the website repo

---

## Blog Post Frontmatter Schema

All blog posts must include these frontmatter fields:

```yaml
---
title: 'Post Title Here'
description: 'Meta description (under 160 characters)'
date: 'YYYY-MM-DD'
dateModified: 'YYYY-MM-DD'
author: 'Nathan Rider'
image: '/images/blog/filename.webp'
imageAlt: 'Descriptive alt text for the image'
tags: ['tag1', 'tag2', 'raleigh']
---
```

- `date` and `dateModified` are strings, not Date objects
- `author` defaults to `Nathan Rider`
- `image` and `imageAlt` are optional but should always be included for blog posts
- Tags should always include `'raleigh'` for local SEO

---

## URL & Routing Conventions

- All page URLs use **trailing slashes** (e.g., `/blog/post-slug/`)
- URL slugs use **hyphens**, never underscores
- Use **`crawl-space`** (hyphenated) in slugs, never `crawlspace` (one word)
- Redirects are handled two ways:
  - **Cloudflare edge redirects** тЖТ `public/_redirects` (preferred, handles both trailing-slash variants)
  - **Astro redirects** тЖТ `astro.config.mjs` (non-trailing-slash only, to avoid route collisions)
- When adding a new redirect, add it to **both** `public/_redirects` (both slash variants) and `astro.config.mjs` (non-slash variant only)

---

## BaseLayout Props

```ts
{
  title: string;
  description: string;
  canonical?: string;      // relative path e.g. '/blog/post-slug/' тАФ trailing slash required
  heroImage?: string;      // path to OG/hero image
  noindex?: boolean;       // set true for /thank-you, /404, /privacy
  breadcrumbs?: { name: string; url: string }[];
}
```

- The canonical URL is auto-constructed as `https://www.triangledehumidifiers.com{canonical}`
- Always pass `canonical` on every page to avoid duplicate content issues
- Breadcrumbs render as JSON-LD `BreadcrumbList` schema automatically

---

## Do Not Touch Without Asking

- `public/_headers` тАФ security headers and cache rules for Cloudflare; changes affect site security
- `public/_redirects` тАФ adding is fine; never remove existing redirects (SEO risk)
- `public/robots.txt` тАФ do not modify
- `astro.config.mjs` тАФ Vite/build config is tuned to inline all CSS/assets; don't change without understanding the reason

---

## SEO Conventions

- Every page needs a unique `<title>` and `<meta description>`
- Meta descriptions should be under 160 characters
- Blog post titles follow the pattern: `Topic | Triangle Dehumidifiers` or informational headline
- Internal links use relative paths (e.g., `/crawl-space-dehumidifier-raleigh-nc/`)
- Images must have descriptive `alt` text; hero/first image uses `loading="eager"`, all others `loading="lazy"`
- Schema markup (LocalBusiness, FAQ, BreadcrumbList) is used site-wide; don't remove schema blocks

---
